license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.re6d3he
title: Phenol-chloroform DNA purification
author: Tomasz Suchan

steps_raw.instruction: To 100ul eluted DNA, add 0.5 ul of 20% SDS (*this may not be necessary as there are no cells) and 100 ul of phenol-chloroform.
Centrifuge at room temperature for 5 min, at full speed (14,000 rpm).
Pipette the aqueous phase (upper phase, aprox 80 ul, it is better to leave some DNA than to pipette phenol) to a new labeled tube.
Discard original tube. The tube contains phenol-chloroform.

Add 1/10 volume Na acetate 3M pH 4.8 or 5.2 (ie 8ul for 80 ul DNA solution) add 2 volumes ETOH 100% (storage -20°C) = 176 ul Total volume: 264ul, possible with 264 ng of starting DNA - otherwise, you must add a carrier: tRNA, glycogen or linear acrylamide.
Put on dry ice for 30 min. or overnight at -20°C
Centrifuge at 4°C for 30 min at 14,000 rpm.
Centrifuge at 4°C for 5 min at 14,000 rpm.
Pipette out the last drop of EtOH
Speed Vac for 3' or 5-7 min at room temperature.
Resuspend in 20ul of water ot Tris 10 mM pH 7.5 or 8.0
